interpretation: The Basic Law's inviolable human dignity provision (Art. 1 GG) and eternity clause (Art. 79(3) GG) are direct constitutional embodiments of high corrigibility: the postwar symbolic order structurally limits its own capacity for pathological escalation by placing individual dignity beyond collective override.

confidence.label: moderate-high
confidence.rationale: Interpretation is well-grounded in the constitutional text and contrasts cleanly with the Nazi Germany sacrificial-escalation case. Chain is currently a minimal stub.

mechanismSummary: The founding constitutional document embeds human dignity as inviolable and unamendable, directly blocking the collective-body logic that characterized the preceding pathological order.
